list.text: x:p,p+9    y: 25,-35                                           The table gives the coordinates of two ‚Å£‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Å§points on a line in the ùë• ùë¶ xy-plane. The ùë¶ y-intercept of the line is  (p‚àí3,c), where  p and  c are constants. What is the value of c?

the y intercept is stated to be at (p‚àí3,c). Since the x value of a y intercept is always 0, the value of p can be found by setting p-3 equal to zero. p-3=0, p=3. This can be used to find the x values of the coordinates in the table. Using p=3, the coordinates in the table are  x:3,12  y:25,-35. y=mx+b, so the slope formula (y2-y1)/(x2-x1) can be used to find the slope, and then the slope and a point on the table can be used to find the b value, which is equal to the y intercept. (-35-25)/(12-3)=-20/3. 25=(-20/3)(3)+b b=45. therefore, the value of c is 45, as the b value in y=mx+b is equal to the y value of the y intercept.

after the value of p has been solved for, a table on desmos can be made. the values put in the table are; x:p,p+9  y: 25,-35. in another expression, define p=3. Then, create a linear regression from the table, as the problem defines the equation as a line. The y value of the y-intercept of the linear regression graph is equal to c.
